Code fragment: public final TableField<LavalinkNodeRecord, Boolean> ENABLED = createField(DSL.name("enabled"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN.defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("true"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN)), this, "");
Explanation: The 'enabled' column has a default value of 'true' but is marked as NULLABLE (no .nullable(false) specified), which means it can contain NULL values even though it has a meaningful default. Consider adding .nullable(false) to ensure the column always has a valid boolean value.

Code fragment: public final TableField<MembersRecord, Long> GUILD_ID = createField(DSL.name("guild_id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The GUILD_ID column appears to reference the Guild table (similar to how EVENT_LOG and RECENT_TRACKS reference it) but lacks a foreign key constraint, which could lead to orphaned records. Add a foreign key constraint referencing Guild.GUILD_ID to ensure referential integrity.

Code fragment: public final TableField<MembersRecord, Boolean> IS_ADMIN = createField(DSL.name("is_admin"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN.defaultValue(DSL.field(DSL.raw("false"), SQLDataType.BOOLEAN)), this, "");
Explanation: The IS_ADMIN column has a default value but is marked as NULLABLE, which is inconsistent since a column with a default value should typically be NOT NULL. Add a NOT NULL constraint to this column to prevent ambiguous NULL values.
